transcript.text: Hey, how are you doing?
Good, good.
How are you doing here in the office?
Yeah, yeah, in the office today.
So, yeah, just, you know, hanging out.
How are you doing?
Yeah, pretty good.
Managed to edit some stuff for execs.
yeah, pretty busy, you know, preparing for everything.
So, pretty good.
Yeah, for sure.
Hey, Natalie.
Hi, everyone.
How's it going?
I'm sure you just caught up before.
Only briefly.
How are you doing?
Hi.
Hi.
I put together some meeting notes.
And I tagged you all in Notion, but in case you didn't see, I'm just going to share in the chat for Don anyway, once he joins us.
Cool.
Aida, how's your face?
It's better today.
It's a lot better, but still not 100%.
I think tomorrow I'm going to be better.
Are you in the office every day, Megan?
No, no.
Yeah, I'm trying to go in like once a week.
But yeah, definitely not, definitely not every day.
I would love to have an office actually.
It's kind of funny because like remote is like a very much of a preferred word for most people, but I'd like to go to the office sometimes or have a chance at least.
Yeah, yeah.
It is nice to get out of the house every now and then.
So, yeah.
Well, I guess let me just check.
I know Don is coming, but he was reviewing a lot of our article deliveries, so.
Hello, hello.
Hi, hi.
Hi, Don.
Love the new faces.
How's everybody doing?
Good.
you.
All right, did everybody have a good weekend?
Yeah, I had a good weekend.
Yeah, I played some softball.
So, yeah, what about you?
Oh, nice, softball.
I never played softball, I think.
I had a good weekend as well.
Just had some beautiful weekend there in SF.
So, was interesting that, like, we get in, like, October, beginning of October, it's the best time of the year here.
Yeah, oh, nice.
Where in San Francisco are you?
Which neighborhood?
I'm based in Mission Bay.
Oh, okay, nice.
And the office is in Soma.
Okay, nice.
Yeah, I'm in the office today.
We are in South Park, so it's not too far.
Yeah, it's close.
So, just to kind of kick off our meeting, I mean, as you saw, Dawn, I bien We're
Yep, a couple new faces today.
This is Jo and Megan, who will be leading the team that will take over Alex after this week and taking over, you know, content publishing, ideation, strategy.
And like Aida mentioned, we'll still, you know, be here for support or, you know, just sharing context with the team through the transition to make sure that everything is good.
But yeah, if you have questions, of course, you still have access to us, so feel free to reach out.
So, yeah, I'll let Jo and Megan introduce themselves, explain their process, and what that will look like for you.
Perfect.
Cool.
Megan, do you want to start?
Yeah, yeah, I can just jump in.
Yeah, yeah, it's good to meet you, Dawn.
Yeah, I'm Megan.
I'm a director here at GrowthX.

And so between Jo and myself, we'll be supporting Alex in terms of strategy, making sure content production is staying on track, you know, reporting analytics, whether it's through our Looker dashboard, but actually soon we'll actually have.
I have our own analytics platform, but then also looking at LLM referrals.
And yeah, just I guess a bit about myself.
My background is actually in journalism.
So I spent almost like 15 years writing at places like TechCrunch and Business Insider and Axios.
So I kind of approach everything from more of like a content perspective.
And Jo is truly like an analytics guru.
So she loves diving into the data or I guess if you don't love it, you're at least very good at it.
So that's definitely a good team.
Yeah, Jo, I'll throw it to you.
Yeah, thank you.
Yeah, so I'm a managing editor here at Growthx.
I'm happy to take the account, especially that my background is in B2B SaaS.
I've worked in the past with many AI-driven startups.
So happy to dive into like Alex's strategy and the editorial side aspect I mentioned to a lot of like SEO stuff around reporting.
So if you have any questions, any, you know, specific parts of the website that you would like to explore more, you can always ask us about all this.
And, yeah, I'm happy to kick things off.
And that's it.
Awesome.
Well, great.
I'm looking forward to working with you both.
Thank you, Cool.
So just to kind of recap what we've been working on, we have three blogs that have been published so far.
They've also been indexed by Google.
So that means they're appearing, you know, correctly when people are searching for those terms and they're related to your website.
So it looks like Google is automatically indexing those.
So you won't have to necessarily submit them to be manually indexed.
And as mentioned earlier, we do have a few more blogs ready for review.
I saw that you started editing them and sending them back for revision.
So we'll work on that.
Thank
This week, and I just wanted to double check, is it more useful for you to get notifications for new articles via email or Slack?
I think last week we discussed testing out email, but I wanted to check if that still works for you or if you'd prefer something else.
I mean, if I can, I think, like, maybe the Slack is probably the best way.
Okay.
like a quick note that they're, that new have, no one is ready for me to review it, but I can just go and quickly check it.
Yeah.
Okay.
So I'll update the, we have an automation set up through Airtable to do this.
So I'll just update it to send you a Slack message or send a message in the channel and tag you.
Perfect.
And then another question I had, um, in terms of reporting, do you have a specific, any specific key events in GA4 that you'd like us to track for conversions?
I looked at, and I saw, like, the pretty, like, standard.
Key events, and I'm wondering if there's like a specific one related to booking a demo that you'd want us to use.
I think there's like a standard form submit one.
Yeah, nothing other than that.
So I can add a new one if needed, but right now it's just a standard form submit.
Okay, so we can use form submit for now, but I mean, Jo, if you have any references on how to do that moving forward.
I'm sure you and John can discuss and figure out what the best move is.
Yeah, for sure.
Like there's one of the, like one of the tabs in our Looker reporting has this like event dashboard.
So we can modify this reporting a little bit to show, for example, like which specific pages contributes to the key conversion events.
So I think like we can make more data from decisions this way, like observing, you know.
So...
The blog is bringing any specific conversions, but if this event is working properly, then we will focus on this one for sure.
Okay, great.
Dawn, I just shared a link to the Looker dashboard in the chat and also shared on Slack if you want to take a look and see what that would look like.
Let's see, what else?
Just some updates for this week.
We're working on five new blogs along with the revisions from the previously delivered blogs once you start finishing them.
And then also we're finalizing any additional changes to the artifacts and our workflows so that everything is ready for the growth execution stage with Megan and Jo's team.
And I'm wondering, is there anything in terms of product updates or product messaging that you really want to make sure that we highlight in these revisions?
I've collected a bunch of, you know, based on your feedback and previous conversations, notes for this, but I want to make sure that if there's anything that's super important or...
High priority for you that we don't miss that.
And we can make sure that it's highlighted.
Like you're referring to any new product launch that we're doing and having like, what's the best way moving forward to if we have a new product launch to like incorporate that?
Do I just mention it during this meeting or what should be the way of communicating that?
What do you think, Jo?
So, I mean, moving forward, this will be your work.
So I'll leave it at you.
Yeah.
I think like as we have catch-ups every week, you can keep us updated because probably there will be some updates around artifacts, especially like major feature updates.
And then, you know, like we should not really include these new additions to extra hour content.
So definitely just let's keep the feedback loop week over week.
Okay.
Sounds good.
Sounds
Cool.

And then last week we had mentioned if you could share any internal product messaging or feature lists or competitor battle cards just so we can make sure, especially when we're creating content, comparing like Alex against your competitors, making sure that we have everything that's accurate and also maybe highlighting some of the key differentiators that separate Alex from its competitors.

So if you're able to share any of those docs, I'm also, I'll send a follow-up message with all of this in it, but I just wanted to bring that up again in case that was something that you could work on.
Yeah, feel free to send me another reminder about it.
I remember when I asked about it last week to our sales team and we have like pieces of information a bit scattered but not really like anything built out.
It's not really like anything that's done.
Yeah.
Yeah.
It's really.
I mean.
I mean, of course, I don't want to answer for you, Jo, whatever works best for you, but I think any information that you can provide would be helpful.

It doesn't have to be like a beautiful presentation or doc or anything, just to make sure that we're really understanding, especially if it comes to competitors or how you want to talk about alex versus competitors, that we're using the right messaging there.
Okay.
Well, and then I don't know that I have really anything else in my to-do to talk about today.
Aida, do you have, is there anything else that you should cover, or Megan or Jo, if you have anything that you want to discuss as we're still in this kind of like last week transition period?
No, I'm all good on my end.
I don't know if Megan and Jo have anything else.
Yeah, I mean, yeah, I don't, I don't think we really have anything for you.
Yeah, we were just really wanting.
And to meet you and just sort of let you know that, yeah, like, Jo and I will be your team moving forward.
But that said, Aida and Nathalie are disappearing.
So, like, they'll, if you still want to talk to them, like, you can.
But we'll still be your kind of first, yeah, first point of contact moving forward.
Sounds good.
Yeah, like, for me, it's just right now I want to get the volume of the content that is published up.
Because right now, like, we've published three posts over, what, two weeks?
And I want to be making sure that we're hitting five a week.
So, like, anything that we can do there to increase that volume, let me know.
Yeah, I think once I complete all the, like, as you start, you know, reviewing the content and I see all the revisions and feedback, I'll start working on that.
And then from there, those will just get moved to publishing.
So this week, we'll definitely see a big increase in publication.
Just last week, we had a little bit of a delay in some deliveries.
But, yeah, I think moving forward.
You'll definitely reach at least five pieces a week.
This week we'll have a few, ideally a few more, just to catch up on what we've been a little bit behind on.
But yeah, I think just in order to move things forward, just be like reviewing things for you to review drafts.
And then on our end, we'll keep up with publishing and revisions as well.
Yeah, perfect.
You'll be in great hands with Jo and Megan.
They're brilliant.
But yeah, of course, if you have any questions, reach out to them or reach out to us if you need.
But yeah, they'll take great care of you.
Awesome.
I appreciate that.
Thank you, Nathalie.
I think the only thing, if we can have like eight at any given time, well, it would be nice to have at least a buffer of like a couple articles that are always ready to go.
So if we can send some to me at any given time, I'll try to review them as quickly as possible.
If you can send some like next week, I'm out.
Yeah.
For a full week at a conference, so if you have any articles that I can review before, like, Friday, that would be helpful.
Okay, yeah, that sounds good.
I know we're already in production.
think at least two of the five planned for this week are already done, so I'll just prioritize editing those so I can get everything to you, hopefully, by Thursday.
That way, that'll give you enough time to review everything before you're out for the conference.
But, yeah, thanks for flagging that for us.

And, yeah, just moving forward, if there's something like that where you kind of want to, you know, fast track things or prioritize things, yeah, just let the team know, because we definitely want to stay up, like, stay on top of publishing as you expect.
Perfect.
Sounds good.
Let's do it.
Cool.
Well, I mean, that's all for me.
If there's no other remaining questions or to-dos for anyone, I just want to thank you for your time.
And it was really wonderful working with you, John.
And I know, like I said, make-
And then, Jo will do an awesome job with your account moving forward.
Likewise, Anlie.
Appreciate everything that you did for us.
And Aida as well.
Thank you.
Thank you so much.
Okay, cool.
Well, thanks, everyone.
Thanks for the super productive meeting.
I'll give you back part of your day.
Thanks, everyone.
Thank you so much.
Ciao, ciao.
Thanks, everyone.
Bye.
Bye.
Bye.

default_summary.markdown_formatted:
## Meeting Purpose

[Weekly sync between Alex and GrowthX to discuss content strategy, team transition, and ongoing projects.](https://fathom.video/share/k35Ygtag47CxThrMG4DALZx2yadwiVgs?tab=summary&timestamp=233.0)

## Key Takeaways

  - [GrowthX team transition: Jo and Megan introduced as new leads for Alex's account](https://fathom.video/share/k35Ygtag47CxThrMG4DALZx2yadwiVgs?tab=summary&timestamp=233.0)
  - [Content production: 3 blogs published and indexed; aiming for 5 posts/week](https://fathom.video/share/k35Ygtag47CxThrMG4DALZx2yadwiVgs?tab=summary&timestamp=388.0)
  - [Reporting: Using form submit event in GA4; Looker dashboard shared for analytics](https://fathom.video/share/k35Ygtag47CxThrMG4DALZx2yadwiVgs?tab=summary&timestamp=465.0)
  - [Product updates: Don to communicate new launches during weekly meetings](https://fathom.video/share/k35Ygtag47CxThrMG4DALZx2yadwiVgs?tab=summary&timestamp=605.0)
  - [Pre-conference prep: Don needs articles to review before Friday](https://fathom.video/share/k35Ygtag47CxThrMG4DALZx2yadwiVgs?tab=summary&timestamp=877.0)

## Topics

### Team Transition

  - [Nathalie and Aida transitioning out; Jo and Megan taking over](https://fathom.video/share/k35Ygtag47CxThrMG4DALZx2yadwiVgs?tab=summary&timestamp=233.0)
  - [Jo (Managing Editor): B2B SaaS background, AI startup experience, SEO focus](https://fathom.video/share/k35Ygtag47CxThrMG4DALZx2yadwiVgs?tab=summary&timestamp=335.0)
  - [Megan (Director): 15 years journalism experience (TechCrunch, Business Insider, Axios)](https://fathom.video/share/k35Ygtag47CxThrMG4DALZx2yadwiVgs?tab=summary&timestamp=276.0)
  - [New team will handle strategy, content production, reporting, and analytics](https://fathom.video/share/k35Ygtag47CxThrMG4DALZx2yadwiVgs?tab=summary&timestamp=276.0)

### Content Production Status

  - [3 blogs published and indexed by Google](https://fathom.video/share/k35Ygtag47CxThrMG4DALZx2yadwiVgs?tab=summary&timestamp=388.0)
  - [Additional blogs ready for review, Don started editing](https://fathom.video/share/k35Ygtag47CxThrMG4DALZx2yadwiVgs?tab=summary&timestamp=411.0)
  - [5 new blogs in production this week](https://fathom.video/share/k35Ygtag47CxThrMG4DALZx2yadwiVgs?tab=summary&timestamp=563.0)
  - [Goal: Increase to 5 posts/week (current: 3 posts in 2 weeks)](https://fathom.video/share/k35Ygtag47CxThrMG4DALZx2yadwiVgs?tab=summary&timestamp=802.0)
  - [Don requests 8 articles buffer; team to prioritize 2 finished articles for review by Thursday](https://fathom.video/share/k35Ygtag47CxThrMG4DALZx2yadwiVgs?tab=summary&timestamp=877.0)

### Reporting and Analytics

  - [Switching notifications from email to Slack for new article reviews](https://fathom.video/share/k35Ygtag47CxThrMG4DALZx2yadwiVgs?tab=summary&timestamp=420.0)
  - [Using standard form submit event in GA4 for conversions](https://fathom.video/share/k35Ygtag47CxThrMG4DALZx2yadwiVgs?tab=summary&timestamp=465.0)
  - [Looker dashboard shared; potential for customized event tracking](https://fathom.video/share/k35Ygtag47CxThrMG4DALZx2yadwiVgs?tab=summary&timestamp=516.0)
  - [Jo to modify reporting to show page-specific conversion contributions](https://fathom.video/share/k35Ygtag47CxThrMG4DALZx2yadwiVgs?tab=summary&timestamp=516.0)

### Product Updates and Messaging

  - [Don to communicate new product launches during weekly meetings](https://fathom.video/share/k35Ygtag47CxThrMG4DALZx2yadwiVgs?tab=summary&timestamp=605.0)
  - [Request for internal product messaging, feature lists, and competitor battle cards](https://fathom.video/share/k35Ygtag47CxThrMG4DALZx2yadwiVgs?tab=summary&timestamp=662.0)
  - [Team needs accurate info for content comparing Alex against competitors](https://fathom.video/share/k35Ygtag47CxThrMG4DALZx2yadwiVgs?tab=summary&timestamp=662.0)

### Workflow and Communication

  - [Slack preferred for article review notifications](https://fathom.video/share/k35Ygtag47CxThrMG4DALZx2yadwiVgs?tab=summary&timestamp=420.0)
  - [Weekly meetings to discuss product updates and major feature changes](https://fathom.video/share/k35Ygtag47CxThrMG4DALZx2yadwiVgs?tab=summary&timestamp=605.0)
  - [Don out for conference next week; needs articles to review before Friday](https://fathom.video/share/k35Ygtag47CxThrMG4DALZx2yadwiVgs?tab=summary&timestamp=877.0)

## Next Steps

  - [Nathalie to update Airtable automation for Slack notifications](https://fathom.video/share/k35Ygtag47CxThrMG4DALZx2yadwiVgs?tab=summary&timestamp=420.0)
  - [Jo to customize Looker dashboard for more detailed event tracking](https://fathom.video/share/k35Ygtag47CxThrMG4DALZx2yadwiVgs?tab=summary&timestamp=516.0)
  - [Team to send internal product messaging and competitor info (Don to provide)](https://fathom.video/share/k35Ygtag47CxThrMG4DALZx2yadwiVgs?tab=summary&timestamp=662.0)
  - [Nathalie to prioritize editing 2 finished articles for Don's review by Thursday](https://fathom.video/share/k35Ygtag47CxThrMG4DALZx2yadwiVgs?tab=summary&timestamp=877.0)
  - [Don to review articles before Friday (pre-conference)](https://fathom.video/share/k35Ygtag47CxThrMG4DALZx2yadwiVgs?tab=summary&timestamp=877.0)
  - [New team (Jo and Megan) to focus on increasing content volume to 5 posts/week](https://fathom.video/share/k35Ygtag47CxThrMG4DALZx2yadwiVgs?tab=summary&timestamp=802.0)

action_items.description: Update Airtable automation to send Slack notifs (not email) for new articles
Review prioritized articles before Friday (prep for week out at conference)
Edit & send 2+ articles to Donald by Thu (for pre-conference review)
